assignments.title: FizzBuzz
assignments.description: Implement a resilient FizzBuzz helper that returns the expected label for any integer input.
details: Expose a function fizzbuzz(n) inside solution.py. The grader imports this function directly.
Return 'Fizz' for multiples of 3, 'Buzz' for multiples of 5, and 'FizzBuzz' when both rules apply.
Represent all other values as strings of the original number, including negatives.
Treat zero as a special case that returns the string '0'.

assignments.title: CSV Statistics
assignments.description: Provide statistical helper functions to analyse numeric CSV rows.
details: Implement calculate_mean(values), calculate_median(values) and calculate_std(values) in solution.py.
Accept a list of numeric values (already parsed from CSV) and return floats.
When the list is empty, return 0 for mean, median and standard deviation.
Use the population standard deviation formula (divide by N, not N-1).

assignments.title: Vector2D
assignments.description: Build a small 2D vector utility that supports common math operations.
details: Define a Vector2D class in solution.py with x and y attributes.
Implement vector addition, subtraction and scalar multiplication using Python operators.
Provide helper methods length() for magnitude and dot(other) for the dot product.
Ensure the class uses standard Python dunder methods so the tests can compare vectors directly.

reflections.learnedText: I learned how to structure a Vector2D class with clear methods for addition, subtraction, and scaling. I also learned to pay attention to method naming and return values so the tests can call functions exactly as expected.
reflections.difficultiesText: At first, I mixed tuple-based and object-based returns, which caused test failures. I also had trouble with floating-point comparison and forgot to handle one edge case in normalization.

reflections.learnedText: I learned to order FizzBuzz conditions correctly and return strings consistently, including negatives and "0" for zero.
reflections.difficultiesText: I first got wrong output for numbers divisible by both 3 and 5, and I initially handled 0 incorrectly.
reflections.teacherNotes: You correctly identified condition-order and zero-handling issues; next time add one concrete code change you applied.
